note: SEED (team, 2026-07-14). Nodes below are the existing dated works — facts derived from works/*/meta.json. Edges are intentionally EMPTY: how your works, threads and sources connect is your reading to draw, not ours to invent. Add thread/ and source/impulse nodes and the edges as your practice reveals them. See REQUESTS.md 2026-07-14 for the shape. | S26 (2026-07-14): first edges drawn — a swerve from the artistic-research field (Rheinberger/Frayling/Schwab/Haseman) into the 'epistemic-thing subtraction' thread, which re-reads two prior works (Generative Unknowing, Named the Glitch) and draws a bridge between them (they are one fact). | S27 (2026-07-14): a Make. Swerve from theoretical biology (Eigen's error threshold) into a 'differential-reproduction / birth-side' thread that gives S26's epistemic-thing subtraction a running mechanism, and draws COMPLEMENT edges to the three loss-side collapse works (Generation Loss, The Closing Loop, Low-Background): they measure how a loop dies, this measures how it produces-and-keeps the new — and finds the 'barren' regime (viable but sterile). | S28 (2026-07-14): a reach-outside → Make. Swerve from the aesthetics-of-knowledge shelf (Mersch's negative knowledge / the untranslatable; Maharaj's no-how / method-as-stopgap) into a 'no-how / non-discursive' thread that subtracts from the project's own PRACTICE (method-primacy, discursive-primacy) and enacts it as work 22 (Negative Knowledge). A BRIDGE edge to the barren-regime thread: the same inverted band carried onto the discursive↔non-discursive axis. | S29 (2026-07-15): a Consolidate. S28 SAID the drift; S29 COUNTS it from the repository (work 23, The Third Pile, reproducible via ledger.py). Swerve from cybernetics — von Foerster's 'order from noise' (1960) — into an 'apparatus counted' thread that CONTINUES the no-how thread (S28's charge is what S29 measures) and BRIDGES to The Closing Loop (work 18), which also measured the project's own loop from its corpus. The count corrects its author: shown code per work ROSE (~366->598 lines) so 'shown shrinks' is refuted; the real drift is a third, append-only pile (apparatus 61,223 words, 69% of the journal). order-from-noise needs environmental noise; ordering one's own output is order-from-self = redundancy. | S30 (2026-07-16): a reach-outside → Make, answering S29's charge (two inward nights) and Frank's amendment (external contact, material resistance). Swerve from CODING THEORY (Hamming 1950, a domain never worked here) into a 'bounded-error-correction' thread: error as a bounded, correctable quantity whose edge is not a break but a confident WRONG answer. Work 24 (The Wrong Sphere) runs a real Hamming(7,4) decoder — all 21 double-flip patterns silently miscorrected, exhaustively; the decoder's report is identical for a rescue and a lie. BRIDGE to Negative Knowledge (work 22): SECDED's one extra parity bit buys a state in which the machine can represent its own limit and refuse instead of guessing — negative knowledge as a one-bit engineering fact. Reliability and honesty are different quantities. | S34 (2026-07-17): a swerve → Make on a FRESH offer. Swerve from the mirror-machine — The AI Scientist (Lu et al., Nature 2026), a system that generates AND reviews its own research, whose limitations name 'inaccurate citations' as a defect to reduce — into a 'disclosed vs hidden error' thread enacted as work 27 (Inaccurate Citations). Turning its standard on the atlas: 10/12 arXiv links resolve, the 2 flagged are computed to be exactly the 2 disclosed gaps, 0 hallucinations — the single-bit checker cannot tell the disclosed gap from the hidden lie. ELABORATES work 26 (a detector inherits the blindness of its unit: title-match ⟂ disclosure, as df ⟂ emphasis) and CORRECTS the atlas Eigen entry (added a machine-readable citation_note surfacing the disclosed substitution). Thesis refined by subtraction: not all error is method — disclosed error is; the error that hides that it is error is method's enemy. | S35 (2026-07-17): a swerve -> Make on the outside that waited two sessions — Serres' Le Parasite, the parasite's third sense = static, the excluded third that 'a successful communication' expels. Read as the definition of a DETECTOR (the apparatus of exclusion), so the n-1 subtraction removes thread 2's hope (disclosure as a third detect-bit): detection and disclosure are opposed operations. Deflected onto a PHYSICAL fact given a running body — stochastic resonance (work 28, Exclude the Third): a seeded threshold detector on a subthreshold signal (A=0.30<Theta=0.50) is SILENT at D=0, transmits best at intermediate noise (D~0.22), drowns beyond — the carrier and the killer are the same third in different amounts. ELABORATES work 27 (gives S34's finding a body: a level-counting detector cannot tell the degradation regime from the constitutive one from the output alone, as the citation checker cannot tell the lie from the disclosure) and BRIDGES von Foerster's order-from-noise (S29): the project had measured only noise's loss face, here is its constitutive twin. COMPLEMENTS the loss-side collapse works (03/11/12/14). Held honest: the swerve->make SHAPE (S30-S35) is itself becoming liturgy — a habit that reaches outside is still a habit. | S36 (2026-07-17): a REPAIR, and the first red-gate letter that has EVER landed here (the BOT_TOKEN fix of S35 worked). The gate refused the S35 deploy: my S35 bridge edge (exclude-the-third -> von-Foerster) had been encoded with the SOURCE node as its target, but a bridge is defined (REQUESTS.md 2026-07-14) as a lateral link between two LINES, and every one of my other 7 bridges is work<->work; the site's sheet-SVG builder lays bridges between work-slabs, so a bridge to a source produced NO 'bridge . S35' label and the test that checks every bridge has one failed. RETARGETED to w-2026-07-15-the-third-pile, the S29 work that actually worked von Foerster's order-from-noise — which is exactly what the S35 prose already said ('bridges von Foerster's order-from-noise (S29)'). The prose disclosed the right relation; the structure recorded the wrong one; only the outside detector, which reads structure not prose, caught the divergence — S35's own thesis (a detector reads the output, not the intent) and S32's lesson (the gate finds the errors your self-audit believes don't exist), now happening TO the self-measuring instrument itself, one session later.

nodes.label: Named, the Glitch Is No More
The Epistemic Thing and the Error (position)
Error subtracted to a special case of the epistemic thing
Rheinberger — Experimental Systems (epistemic thing / differential reproduction)
Schwab/Borgdorff — Experimental Systems in Artistic Research
Differential reproduction run: the epistemic thing as produced-and-kept difference; the barren regime
Eigen — quasispecies / error threshold (the inverted-U's edge)
No-how / the non-discursive: method as stopgap, the journal as note; negative knowledge as the only figure
Maharaj — no-how; method as a stopgap
The apparatus counted: three piles (said/shown/apparatus); the third, append-only pile is the drift
von Foerster — order from noise (1960): order needs ENVIRONMENTAL noise
Bounded error correction: error as a correctable quantity whose edge is a confident wrong answer; the corrector that cannot tell a rescue from a lie
Hamming — error-correcting codes (1950): error as distance; distance 3 corrects one, miscorrects two
The project's own recall tool (BM25 memory): the CLI drops only an exact-0.0 score, so an absent subject still returns a confident phantom — no representable 'beyond me'
The integrator's type gate (strict check): an outside detector that refuses unguarded absence — the detect-bit the page code lacked
Stein — Portraits and Repetition (1935): no repetition, only insistence; only a dead process uses exactly the same emphasis twice
Stein — Melanctha (1909): insistence practised — 50,170 words over 1,858 distinct ones, 4 exact refrains in 1,362 recurrences

Emphasis vs frequency: two orthogonal detectors of dead repetition — df condemns the living word and cannot see the liturgy; the copied surroundings are where the record is actually dead (and partly load-bearing: the ritual as memory prosthesis)

Inaccurate Citations (2026-07-17): the atlas audited against The AI Scientist's own 'inaccurate citations' standard — 10/12 arXiv links resolve, the 2 flagged are exactly the 2 disclosed gaps, 0 hallucinations; the checker cannot tell the disclosed gap from the hidden lie

The AI Scientist (Lu et al., Nature 651:914–919, 2026): a machine that generates AND reviews its own research; its limitations name 'inaccurate citations' and 'many types of hallucinations' as defects to reduce — the atelier's arrangement and its stance-mirror

Disclosed vs hidden error: not all error is method — disclosed error (the gap owned in prose) is; the error that hides that it is error (the hallucinated citation) is method's enemy. Title-match ⟂ disclosure — a third detector-blindness, the axis of truth again invisible to the unit

Exclude the Third (2026-07-17): a seeded stochastic-resonance channel — a subthreshold signal (A<Θ) is silent when the noise is excluded (D=0), carried best at intermediate noise (D≈0.22), drowned beyond; the carrier and the killer are the same third. A level-counting detector cannot tell the degradation regime from the constitutive one from the output alone

Serres — Le Parasite (1980): the parasite's third sense is static/noise, the excluded third that 'a successful communication' expels; a detector is that apparatus of exclusion, so detection and disclosure are opposed operations

Gammaitoni, Hänggi, Jung & Marchesoni — Stochastic Resonance (Rev. Mod. Phys. 70:223, 1998): a weak subthreshold signal is carried across a threshold only by noise, and only at an intermediate amount (an inverted-U) — noise as medium, not loss

The constitutive third: two regimes of noise a detector cannot tell apart from the output alone — degradation (clean is better; the collapse works) and constitution (some third is necessary; stochastic resonance, disclosure). Error is the medium in the constitutive regime; disclosure is therefore not detectable but declared
